prompt:
A researcher claims that recycling rate in the town climbed steadily from 2020 to 2024.

Which finding from the table most directly supports the claim?

choices.text: The value climbed each year, moving from 40 to 70 percent over the period.
choices.rationale: Correct. This matches the steady, one-direction trend the claim describes across every year shown.

choices.text: It peaked in 2022 at 50 percent and then reversed course for the rest of the period.
choices.rationale: The largest value is at an endpoint, not the middle year.

choices.text: The value changed by the same amount every year, a constant 8 percent per year.
choices.rationale: The year-to-year changes vary, so the change was not a constant 8 percent.

choices.text: In some years the value moved down and in others up, rather than in a single direction.
choices.rationale: Every year moves the same direction, so this misreads the table.

explanation: The supporting statement must reflect the table's actual, consistent trend across all the years shown.

table.caption: Recycling rate in the town (percent)
